text.title: Experimentation Maturity and Platform Stack
text.summary: Defines the four maturity levels for organizational experimentation and the 2026 tool stack required to reach Level 3 (Scaled) with statistical rigor.

list.item: Level 1 — Ad-hoc: individual experiments, no standardization, no shared tooling.
Level 2 — Structured: defined process, shared tools, reproducible setup.
Level 3 — Scaled: 100+ experiments/year, statistical rigor, guardrail metrics on every test.
Level 4 — Culture: every significant product decision is backed by an experiment; results are organization-wide knowledge.

rule.statement: Target Level 3 before claiming "we are an experimentation culture." Level 3 requires: a pre-registration log, a guardrail metric registry, SRM checks on every experiment, and a closed learning repository.
rule.rationale: Organizations claiming culture without Level 3 infrastructure run experiments that cannot be trusted; results are theater, not evidence.

list.item: Feature Flags: LaunchDarkly, Statsig — safe rollouts and kill-switches.
A/B Testing: GrowthBook (developer-first, OSS), Statsig (1T+ events/day), Eppo (warehouse-native, statistical rigor).
Analytics: Amplitude, Mixpanel — behavioral data for hypothesis authoring and readout context.
Warehouse: Snowflake, BigQuery — source of truth for primary metric computation.

rule.statement: Never compute experiment statistics in the analytics tool (Amplitude, Mixpanel). Delegate all statistical computation to the A/B platform (GrowthBook, Statsig, Eppo) which controls assignment randomization and sample integrity.
rule.rationale: Analytics tools are designed for exploration, not causal inference; computing lift in an analytics tool produces biased estimates due to peeking and non-randomized cohort cuts.

list.item: AI-generated test hypotheses from behavioral clusters and support themes.
Automated variant creation for copy and layout tests.
Predictive winner detection to reduce runtime for clear signals.
Natural language result summaries for stakeholder communication.

rule.statement: Use AI to accelerate hypothesis generation and result narration, never to make ship/kill decisions autonomously. The final verdict must be a human-reviewed application of the pre-committed decision rule.
rule.rationale: AI narrators round confidence intervals, paraphrase null results as directionally positive, and defer to the PM's prior. Human review of the verbatim statistical output is the only guard.

section.title: Scale Benchmarks
section.p: The competitive gap between ad-hoc (Level 1) and scaled (Level 3) orgs compounds with each roadmap cycle — Level 3 orgs run 10x more tests and accumulate 10x more validated learnings per year.
